description: Verify real canonical POST /api/memory works in both auto and soc2 modes, collect DB evidence, return pass/fail.

features.description: Verify canonical POST /api/memory in auto mode
steps: Start Next.js server: bun run dev (in background)
Wait for server to be ready: curl http://localhost:3000/api/health
Start with clean state: docker compose down -v && docker compose up -d
Wait for databases to be ready (PostgreSQL + Neo4j)
Run: curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/memory -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"label": "TestInsight", "props": {"test": true}, "group_id": "allura-validation"}'
Capture returned UUID from response
Query PostgreSQL: SELECT id, metadata->>'memory_id' as memory_id FROM events WHERE group_id = 'allura-validation' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
Verify: returned UUID == metadata.memory_id
Verify: events.id is numeric (BIGSERIAL)
Report: PASS or FAIL with evidence
features.notes: This validates the canonical interface in auto mode (no soc2 kernel routing yet)

features.description: Verify canonical POST /api/memory in soc2 mode
steps: Ensure soc2 mode is enabled (check environment or config)
Run: curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/memory -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"label": "TestInsight", "props": {"test": true}, "group_id": "allura-validation-soc2"}'
Capture returned UUID from response
Query PostgreSQL: SELECT id, metadata->>'memory_id' as memory_id, trace_ref FROM events WHERE group_id = 'allura-validation-soc2' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
Verify: returned UUID == metadata.memory_id
Verify: events.id is numeric (BIGSERIAL)
Verify: trace_ref is numeric (BIGINT, foreign key to canonical_proposals)
Report: PASS or FAIL with evidence
features.notes: This validates the canonical interface in soc2 mode (with kernel routing and trace_ref)

steps: Query PostgreSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE group_id LIKE 'allura-validation%'
Query PostgreSQL: SELECT id, group_id, event_type, metadata->>'memory_id' as memory_id, trace_ref FROM events WHERE group_id LIKE 'allura-validation%' ORDER BY created_at
Query Neo4j: MATCH (n) WHERE n.group_id STARTS WITH 'allura-validation' RETURN n
Generate report: total events, memory_ids, trace_refs, Neo4j nodes
Output: <promise>COMPLETE</promise> with full evidence
